info.title: Sentrix API — Safety & Security Platform
info.description:
**Sentrix** is the canonical backend for the Sentrix safety & security platform — one
versioned, multi-tenant API (`/api/v1`) that consolidates personal safety, organizational
security operations, a security-command/CAD layer, evidence & intelligence, and a safe
mobility marketplace, built for the Nigerian market. The **SentrixCore** AI agent is a
separate Python service plugged in over HTTP; this API exposes the bridge to it.

Every endpoint is versioned under `/api/v1`. Responses use a consistent envelope —
`{ success, message, data }` on success and `{ success, message, errors }` on failure.

## Authentication
Laravel Sanctum **bearer tokens**. API clients (mobile, Postman) send a `device_name`
on register/login to receive a token, then pass `Authorization: Bearer <token>`.
Org-scoped routes sit under `/api/v1/organizations/{organization}/…`, consumer routes
under `/api/v1/me/…`, and platform-staff routes under `/api/v1/{ledger,leads,command,rides/admin}`.

## Who it serves (stakeholders)
- **Citizens / consumers** — mobile users protecting themselves and their families: trips,
  live tracking, SOS/emergencies, Safe Rides, community safe-places, and rewards.
- **Organizations / security operators** — security firms, estates, and corporates running
  incident, emergency, and dispatch operations over their own tenant, responders, cameras,
  hardware, and insurance.
- **Responders / field agents** — receive dispatch and taskings, manage duty shifts and skills.
- **Security command & agencies (CAD)** — command hierarchy with GPS routing, CAD units,
  BOLOs, and mutual aid for coordinated, multi-agency response.
- **Platform staff (Sentrix internal)** — operate the financial Ledger, CRM (lead → activation),
  Rides-Ops, and command oversight; gated on elevated platform roles.
- **Partners & integrators** — consume signed webhooks, billing/PSP, and the SentrixCore bridge.

## Key capabilities (what each area does)
- **Identity & Access** — registration/login (device-scoped Sanctum tokens), email
  verification, account & NDPR data export, saved locations, safety contacts, push tokens.
- **Organizations, RBAC & Audit** — multi-tenant organizations, members & invitations,
  roles & granular permissions, ownership transfer, and an immutable audit trail.
- **Incidents, Emergencies & Dispatch** — incident lifecycle, SOS/emergency triggers,
  assignment & dispatch to responders, escalation, and notifications.
- **Trips & Tracking** — trip plans, live location tracking, and overdue/staleness sweeps.
- **Responders** — onboarding, duty shifts, skills, and availability.
- **VisionGuard** — camera registry and media capture.
- **Security Command / CAD** — agencies, command hierarchy + GPS unit routing, CAD
  dispatch, BOLOs, mutual aid, unit comms, command analytics, and taskings.
- **Evidence, Retention & Intel** — forensic evidence vault with search, tiered retention
  lifecycle, and intelligence reports/analytics (with CSV export).
- **Ingest / Signals pipeline** — detection → decision → incident automation, vision /
  SafeSignal ingest, and an anonymized public safety feed.
- **Safe Rides** — ride lifecycle with in-ride safety, driver onboarding & vetting,
  wallet & payments (Sentrix Send), the ride marketplace, surge, and an ops dashboard.
- **Places** — server-side geocode, autocomplete, and category nearby trip-planning proxies.
- **Community & Rewards** — trust-voted safe places and gamified safety engagement.
- **Billing & Webhooks** — subscriptions and PSP checkout, plus signed partner webhook delivery.
- **SentrixCore bridge** — proxy to the Python AI agent: chat (SSE), act, machine event
  ingress, and the command-center view. Degrades gracefully when Core is offline.

> Conventions: UUID identifiers, services-over-controllers with DB transactions, Form-Request
> RBAC, API-Resource response shapes, and broadcast + audited domain events.

post.summary: POST v1/auth/change-password — rotate the password and revoke other tokens

get.summary: GET v1/auth/me/export — NDPR right of access. Read-only

profile.description:
resolve() (not toArray()) so Laravel strips any whenLoaded()
MissingValue — e.g. the nested organization is absent for a
consumer with no org, and toArray() would leave a MissingValue
that fatals on serialization.

delete.summary: DELETE v1/auth/me — NDPR erasure. 422 if the caller still owns a tenant

get.summary: The authenticated user with their org + role context

post.summary:
Staff/Core publish a trusted OFFICIAL / AI alert (verified on arrival).
SuperAdmin-gated in PublishAlertRequest::authorize()

Trust-weighted verify (confirm it's still happening). Raises the alert's
confidence and can flip an unverified alert active

Trust-weighted dispute (flag inaccurate). Lowers the alert's confidence and
can resolve (drop) it past the dispute threshold

A citizen marks a COMMUNITY alert no-longer-active. Official/AI alerts are
managed by staff — citizens cannot resolve them

message.example: Only Sentrix Staff can clear official or AI alerts.

post.description:
FAIL-SAFE: if Core is unconfigured or unreachable, return a valid (200)
text/event-stream carrying a minimal offline fallback, mirroring the Go
mock's graceful degrade — never a 500.
post.summary: POST /api/v1/core/chat — proxy a chat turn to Core and stream its SSE back

post.description:
FAIL-SAFE: if Core is unconfigured or errors, return an explicit "offline"
payload — never fabricate success for a (potentially high-stakes) action.
post.summary: POST /api/v1/core/act — JSON proxy of a confirmed tool action

TODO: gate on the `command_center` / `monitor` roles once those exist;

get.summary:
GET /api/v1/core/command-center — read-only situational snapshot for the
platform (HQ) view. No state change, no event, no Resource: just a plain
`data` array of counts (cf. the skill's computed-action pattern)

post.summary:
POST /api/v1/core/events — a product/detection posts an event. Broadcast a
proactive alert to the org's private channel. Service-token authed (not a
user). Returns 202 Accepted

authenticated user but is assumed to be acting driver-side (no driver
identity yet — that lands with the Driver domain)

the offer. Must be the offer owner (404 otherwise) and the offer must be open

message.const: Top-up initiated. Complete payment to credit your wallet.

note.const: Transfer the exact amount; the wallet is credited when the bank confirms (PSP webhook in production).
Dial the USSD string on the phone linked to your bank; confirmation arrives via the PSP webhook.
Complete payment on the hosted checkout; the PSP webhook confirms the top-up.
Use the reference as the transfer narration; confirmation arrives via the PSP webhook.

402.description:
HTTP 402 so the app can prompt an inline top-up for the shortfall.
The WrapApiResponse error envelope preserves `errors`, so the
shortfall rides there: { success:false, message, errors:{shortfall_cents} }.

ActOnIncidentRequest.description:
Act on a command incident. PLATFORM-scoped: gated on SuperAdmin.

TODO: accept command roles (dispatch_coordinator/monitor) once a platform-staff
RBAC layer exists.
ActOnIncidentRequest.title: ActOnIncidentRequest

last4.description: Store the last 4 digits ONLY — never accept a full PAN.

ArchiveExportRequest.description:
Bundle archive-eligible observations into an export manifest and seal them.
Accepts an OPTIONAL explicit `observation_ids` list; when omitted the service
archives the default eligible set (cold, non-hold, non-sealed).
ArchiveExportRequest.title: ArchiveExportRequest

BookInspectionRequest.description: Driver-scoped: book an in-person inspection slot.
BookInspectionRequest.title: BookInspectionRequest

BookmarkRequest.description:
Bookmark / un-bookmark an observation. Reuses the evidence.hold ability
(curation is a custodial action). The `bookmarked` flag is optional — omit it
to flip the current state.
BookmarkRequest.title: BookmarkRequest

ChangePasswordRequest.description:
Rotate the password for a signed-in consumer (re-auth with the current one).
Mirrors the SentrixGo POST /auth/password/change contract.
ChangePasswordRequest.title: ChangePasswordRequest

ChangeResponderStatusRequest.description:
Authorization is intentionally permissive here (any authenticated org member);
the controller decides between self-service (`responders.self`) and managing
another responder (`responders.manage`), because the rule depends on whether
the target profile belongs to the caller.
ChangeResponderStatusRequest.title: ChangeResponderStatusRequest

distance_m.description: Present only on nearby queries (selectRaw distance_m), in metres.

ConvertPointsRequest.description:
Convert points into a Premium pack. The pack must be one of the configured
premium packs; sufficiency is enforced in the service (422 on shortfall).
ConvertPointsRequest.title: ConvertPointsRequest

CoreEventRequest.description:
Validates an inbound product/detection event posted to /api/v1/core/events.

Authentication is the service-token middleware (`core.service`), not a user —
so authorize() is unconditional here; the trust boundary lives in
{@see \App\Domains\Core\Http\Middleware\AuthenticateCoreService}.
CoreEventRequest.title: CoreEventRequest

CreateAgencyRequest.description:
Onboard a responder agency. PLATFORM-scoped: gated on SuperAdmin.

TODO: accept command roles (dispatch_coordinator/monitor) once a platform-staff
RBAC layer exists.
CreateAgencyRequest.title: CreateAgencyRequest

Issue a BOLO broadcast. PLATFORM-scoped: gated on SuperAdmin.

TODO: accept command roles (dispatch_coordinator/monitor) once a platform-staff
RBAC layer exists.
CreateBoloRequest.title: CreateBoloRequest

Create a command node. PLATFORM-scoped: gated on SuperAdmin.

TODO: accept command roles (dispatch_coordinator/monitor) once a platform-staff
RBAC layer exists.
CreateCommandRequest.title: CreateCommandRequest

Create a field unit. PLATFORM-scoped: gated on SuperAdmin.

TODO: accept command roles (dispatch_coordinator/monitor) once a platform-staff
RBAC layer exists.
CreateUnitRequest.title: CreateUnitRequest

decision.description: The terminal decision states a claim may be moved into.

DispatchUnitRequest.description:
Dispatch (assign) a unit to a command incident. PLATFORM-scoped: gated on
SuperAdmin.

TODO: accept command roles (dispatch_coordinator/monitor) once a platform-staff
RBAC layer exists.
DispatchUnitRequest.title: DispatchUnitRequest

rating_avg.description:
A whole-number decimal serialises without a trailing .0; tests assert
the int/string form. Expose the cast value (decimal:2 string) as-is.

ForceCancelRequest.description:
Force-cancel a ride. SuperAdmin-gated (platform staff).

TODO: replace with a rides:ops / rides:dispatch platform-staff role.
ForceCancelRequest.title: ForceCancelRequest

HoldRequest.description:
Place or release a legal hold on an observation. The `hold` flag is optional —
omit it to flip the current state.
HoldRequest.title: HoldRequest

IngestDetectionRequest.description:
Validates a native detection posted to /api/v1/ingest/detections.

Authentication is the service-token middleware (`core.service`), not a user —
authorize() is unconditional; the middleware is the trust boundary.
IngestDetectionRequest.title: IngestDetectionRequest

fixes.description: Bounded batch so a single request can't be unboundedly large.

IngestResponderLocationsRequest.description:
Authorization is decided in the controller (self vs. manage) because it
depends on whether the target responder profile is the caller's own.
IngestResponderLocationsRequest.title: IngestResponderLocationsRequest

IngestSignalRequest.description:
Validates a SafeSignal report posted to /api/v1/signal/ingest.

The tenant may be referenced as a UUID (organization_id) OR an org slug — both
arrive as a string; the controller resolves it with a Str::isUuid guard. So we
only require *a* tenant reference, not a uuid shape.

Authentication is the service-token middleware (`core.service`), not a user.
IngestSignalRequest.title: IngestSignalRequest

IngestTripLocationsRequest.description:
Batch location fixes for a consumer's own trip (mirrors the operational
tracking ingest contract).
IngestTripLocationsRequest.title: IngestTripLocationsRequest

IngestVisionRequest.description:
Validates a vision-provider payload posted to /api/v1/ingest/vision.

Authentication is the service-token middleware (`core.service`), not a user.
IngestVisionRequest.title: IngestVisionRequest

IngestWriteRequest.description:
Ingest a write reported by a source. Authentication is performed upstream by
the ledger.key middleware (X-Ledger-Key), which binds the resolved source onto
the request — so this request authorizes unconditionally.
IngestWriteRequest.title: IngestWriteRequest

payload.description:
Named `payload` (not `data`) to avoid colliding with the JsonResource
`data` wrapper — a top-level `data` key makes the resource look
already-wrapped and the real attributes get dropped from the envelope.

ObserveRequest.description: Batch ingest of observations into the vault (max 100 per call).
ObserveRequest.title: ObserveRequest

OnboardSourceRequest.description:
Onboard a Ledger source. PLATFORM-scoped: gated on SuperAdmin, not an
organization permission.
OnboardSourceRequest.title: OnboardSourceRequest

emergency_id.description: Cross-references validated against the organization in the controller.

PublishAlertRequest.description:
Publish a trusted OFFICIAL / AI-sourced alert. Staff-only — gated on the
platform SuperAdmin role (no permission-catalogue entry required).
PublishAlertRequest.title: PublishAlertRequest

ReassignRequest.description:
Manual dispatch override. SuperAdmin-gated (platform staff).

TODO: replace with a rides:ops / rides:dispatch platform-staff role.
ReassignRequest.title: ReassignRequest

RecordDecisionRequest.description:
Staff-scoped: record the overall document-review decision for a driver.
PLATFORM-scoped — gated on SuperAdmin, not an organization permission.

TODO: replace with platform-staff 'staff:drivers' role.
RecordDecisionRequest.title: RecordDecisionRequest

RecordInspectionRequest.description:
Staff-scoped: record an in-person inspection outcome (and, on pass, install
the Sentrix hardware kit + activate). PLATFORM-scoped — gated on SuperAdmin.

TODO: replace with platform-staff 'staff:drivers' role.
RecordInspectionRequest.title: RecordInspectionRequest

RegisterDriverRequest.description: Driver-scoped: any authenticated user may register as a driver.
RegisterDriverRequest.title: RegisterDriverRequest

RequestMutualAidRequest.description:
Platform-staff (SuperAdmin) action for now.
TODO: accept command roles (dispatch_coordinator) once a platform-staff RBAC layer exists.
RequestMutualAidRequest.title: RequestMutualAidRequest

ReviewDocumentRequest.description:
Staff-scoped: decide on a single driver document. PLATFORM-scoped — gated on
SuperAdmin, not an organization permission.

TODO: replace with platform-staff 'staff:drivers' role.
ReviewDocumentRequest.title: ReviewDocumentRequest

RouteIncidentRequest.description:
Route an incident to its lead command. PLATFORM-scoped: gated on SuperAdmin.

TODO: accept command roles (dispatch_coordinator/monitor) once a platform-staff
RBAC layer exists.
RouteIncidentRequest.title: RouteIncidentRequest

SetSurgeRequest.description:
Pin or release the manual surge. SuperAdmin-gated (platform staff).

TODO: replace with a rides:ops / rides:dispatch platform-staff role.

multiplier.description: Required to pin; ignored on release.

SetSurgeRequest.title: SetSurgeRequest

proficiency.description: Present only when loaded via a responder's skills() pivot.

StartConsumerTripRequest.description:
Consumer trip start (user-scoped, ADR-0001). The monitored user is always the
authenticated user; the serving org is resolved server-side.
StartConsumerTripRequest.title: StartConsumerTripRequest

user_id.description: Optional: operators may start a trip for another member.

origin_lat.description:
Coordinates must be supplied as a complete lat/lng pair. (No
`sometimes`: it would skip `required_with` when the field is absent.)

items.description: Permissions are anchored to the `web` guard (all RBAC is).

SuspendDriverRequest.description:
Suspend a driver. SuperAdmin-gated (platform staff).

TODO: replace with a rides:ops / rides:dispatch platform-staff role.
SuspendDriverRequest.title: SuspendDriverRequest

TransferOwnershipRequest.description:
Ownership transfer is reserved to the current owner (or a platform SuperAdmin)
— it is not delegable via a permission.
TransferOwnershipRequest.title: TransferOwnershipRequest

trip_id.description: Must reference a trip in the same organization (checked in the controller).

TriggerSosRequest.description:
Consumer SOS trigger (user-scoped). No organization — the serving org is
resolved server-side (ADR-0001).
TriggerSosRequest.title: TriggerSosRequest

UpdateUnitRequest.description:
Partial update of a field unit (AVL / status / capabilities / area). All
fields optional. PLATFORM-scoped: gated on SuperAdmin.

TODO: accept command roles (dispatch_coordinator/monitor) once a platform-staff
RBAC layer exists.
UpdateUnitRequest.title: UpdateUnitRequest

UploadDocumentRequest.description: Driver-scoped: the authenticated driver uploads a document.
UploadDocumentRequest.title: UploadDocumentRequest

errors.description: A detailed description of each field that failed validation.
